hypothesis: Introducing an aromatic residue at position 2 (Y) and a medium‑sized hydrophobic residue at position 8 (M) will increase binding affinity by enhancing π–π and hydrophobic interactions with SSTR2, while maintaining backbone stability.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: Incorporating a pLDDT constraint and relaxing the clash threshold will reduce structural failures, improve docking geometry, and increase the proportion of candidates that meet all QC gates.

proposed_changes.rationale: All top candidates have pLDDT=0.0, indicating low structural confidence. Adding a pLDDT constraint will penalize designs with poor predicted structure quality and encourage more reliable folds.
The current clash threshold is too strict, causing 4 candidates to fail the clash gate. Raising the limit will allow more designs to pass while still monitoring for excessive steric clashes.
